dublinCore.dc:title: 119 HR 9214 IH: TSP Modernization Act
dublinCore.dc:rights: Pursuant to Title 17 Section 105 of the United States Code, this file is not subject to copyright protection and is in the public domain.

action.action-desc: Mr. Bost (for himself, Mrs. Bice, Mrs. Cammack, Mr. Crawford, Mr. DesJarlais, Mr. Donalds, Mr. Ezell, Mr. Guest, Mr. Hurd of Colorado, Mr. Issa, Ms. King-Hinds, Mr. McGuire, Mr. Moore of West Virginia, Mr. Ogles, Mrs. Radewagen, Ms. Salazar, Mr. Stauber, Mr. Van Orden, and Mrs. Wagner) introduced the following bill; which was referred to the Committee on Oversight and Government Reform

form.official-title: To provide for the electronic transfer of amounts from Thrift Savings Fund accounts to qualified retirement plans, and for other purposes.

section.text: This Act may be cited as the TSP Modernization Act.

section.header: Electronic transfer of amounts in Thrift Savings Fund accounts to qualified retirement plans

paragraph.text: Notwithstanding any other law, rule, or regulation, beginning on the date that is 1 year after the date of the enactment of this Act, if a transfer of funds is otherwise permitted from an individual’s account within the Thrift Savings Fund to a qualified retirement plan at a brokerage firm, such transfer shall be permitted to be made electronically.

Such electronic transfer may not be made until such individual provides the information necessary to the Executive Director of the Federal Retirement Thrift Investment Board to carry out such transfer.

subsection.text: On the date that is 1 year after the date of the enactment of this Act, the Executive Director, in consultation with the Secretary of the Treasury, shall submit a report to Congress on the implementation of subsection (a).

paragraph.text: the term Thrift Savings Fund means the Thrift Savings Fund established under section 8437 of title 5, United States Code; and
the term qualified retirement plan has the meaning given that term in section 4974(c) of the Internal Revenue Code of 1986.
